channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: New Dreadquarters Kickstarter Campaign Unleashes Your Inner Evil Villain
item.content:encoded:
A new board game campaign has gone live on Kickstarter. The project, called Dreadquarters, is the first project by Headlock Games on the platform, is a board game where you play as the villain trying to prevent a heroic group from thwarting your plans.

According to the campaign page, Dreadquarters is a cooperative board game. 1-4 players are the newest members of the Dreadquarters Villainous Operations Facility, a fortress designed to give protection and resources to any malicious villains in their wall. But if any wing of this fortress becomes compromised to meddling adventuring groups, the entire place could collapse.

Players choose one of several villainous archetypes. These include the mischievous Unseelie Court, the appropriately vulpine Pack, The Necromancer, The Institute, an so on. Each archetype has their own deck of cards, which holds a unique play style and art style.

These cards will need to be used strategically in different phases of the game. The players will move between two phases of play. The first is Build, which involves building their lair, setting down room tiles, minions, and traps, or raiding the nearby city fro supplies. The second is Defend, where heroes enter your lair to thwart your plans and, hopefully, fall to your defenses.

The wrinkle is that Dreadquarters also has an element of social deduction at play as well. Each villain archetype also has a unique win condition tied to their Grand Scheme, similar to Disney Villainous. If you want to win the game, you have to balance working with your teammates to stop the heroes while completing your Grand Scheme in secret.

There are only three pledge tiers for the Dreadquarters board game. $50 will secure you the base version of the game as well as Kickstarter-exclusive foil room cards. $139 will get you the Deluxe Edition, which includes the base game, foil room cards, full art tuck boxes, bags of tokens, improved token materials, and two expansions with The Hunger and The Marked villain archetypes.

If you are particularly interested in the campaign, $325 is for the All-In Pledge. This gets you everything in the Deluxe Edition, a neoprene play mat, a Forgotten Relics deck and sticker set, a set of pins, an art book, a set of art prints, and a plush of Insolence, a smiling, blood-stained coffee mug.

At the time of writing, the Dreadquarters Kickstarter campaign has raised $31,361, almost three times its initial funding goal of $12,000. The campaign ends on June 12 and products are expected to ship on September 2027.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/new-dreadquarters-kickstarter-campaign-unleashes-your-inner-evil-villain
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
